entities.name: SPO (Self-Supervised Prompt Optimization)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: SPO optimizes prompts without labeled data or references, using LLM-based pairwise comparison of outputs to evaluate and iteratively improve prompts at low cost.

entities.name: LLMLingua-2
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: LLMLingua-2 is a task-agnostic prompt-compression method that uses data distillation to train a token-classification compressor, removing redundant tokens while faithfully preserving essential information for faster inference.

entities.name: LongLLMLingua
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: LongLLMLingua is a prompt-compression technique for long-context scenarios that reduces cost and latency while mitigating the 'lost in the middle' problem to improve LLM performance.

entities.name: Everything of Thoughts (XoT)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Everything of Thoughts (XoT) is a thought-generation prompting paradigm that injects external knowledge via Monte Carlo Tree Search and reinforcement learning to improve performance, efficiency, and flexibility over prior CoT/ToT approaches.

entities.name: Skeleton-of-Thought (SoT)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Skeleton-of-Thought (SoT) first has the LLM produce a skeleton (outline) of the answer, then expands each point in parallel via batched/parallel decoding to cut generation latency.

entities.name: Many-Shot In-Context Learning
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Studies many-shot in-context learning — scaling demonstrations to hundreds or thousands enabled by long context — and introduces Reinforced and Unsupervised ICL variants that use model-generated rationales in place of human labels.

entities.name: Contextual Calibration (Calibrate Before Use)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Contextual calibration estimates an LLM's bias using content-free inputs and adjusts output probabilities to reduce instability across few-shot prompt and example choices.

entities.name: PAL (Program-aided Language Models)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: PAL prompts the LLM to generate a program as the intermediate reasoning steps and offloads the actual computation to a runtime (e.g., a Python interpreter).

entities.name: Set-of-Mark Prompting (SoM)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Set-of-Mark (SoM) prompting uses segmentation to partition an image into regions overlaid with marks (numbers/boxes) so multimodal models like GPT-4V achieve stronger visual grounding.

entities.name: Multimodal Chain-of-Thought (Multimodal-CoT)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Multimodal-CoT is a two-stage chain-of-thought framework that separates rationale generation from answer inference and incorporates both text and vision to improve multimodal reasoning.

entities.name: Batch Prompting
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Batch Prompting groups multiple samples into a single prompt so an LLM performs inference in batches instead of one sample at a time, reducing token and time cost per sample.

entities.name: Structured Prompting
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Structured Prompting scales in-context learning to thousands of examples by encoding grouped demonstrations with position embeddings and a rescaled attention mechanism, bypassing normal context-length limits.

entities.name: Decomposed Prompting (DecomP)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Decomposed Prompting (DecomP) is a modular approach that decomposes a complex task into simpler sub-tasks, each delegated to a dedicated prompting-based LLM in a shared library.

entities.name: Generated Knowledge Prompting
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Generated Knowledge Prompting improves commonsense reasoning by first prompting an LLM to generate relevant knowledge statements, then conditioning the answer on that generated knowledge, without task-specific training.

entities.name: Prefix-Tuning
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Prefix-Tuning is a parameter-efficient alternative to fine-tuning that freezes the LM and optimizes a small continuous task-specific 'prefix' vector prepended to the input for generation tasks.

entities.name: Prompt Tuning (soft prompts)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: The Power of Scale for Parameter-Efficient Prompt Tuning introduces 'soft prompts' learned via backpropagation to condition a frozen LM, matching full fine-tuning as model scale grows.

entities.name: Rephrase and Respond (RaR)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Rephrase and Respond (RaR) is a prompting method in which the LLM rephrases and expands the human's question and then answers it within a single prompt to improve comprehension and accuracy.

entities.name: Progressive Prompts
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Progressive Prompts is a continual-learning method that learns a new soft prompt per task and progressively appends it while freezing the base model, enabling forward transfer without catastrophic forgetting or data replay.

entities.name: Synthetic Prompting
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Synthetic Prompting uses a few seed examples plus a backward-and-forward process to automatically generate and select additional chain-of-thought demonstrations for reasoning tasks.

entities.name: Scratchpad (Show Your Work)
entities.description: Verified from the arXiv abstract: Show Your Work introduces the 'scratchpad' technique, prompting/training LMs to emit intermediate computation steps into a scratchpad before the final answer, improving multi-step reasoning.
